clusters.title: Norwegian crown princess's son found guilty of two counts of rape
clusters.neutral_headline: Norwegian Crown Princess Son Found Guilty

items.title: Norwegian crown princess's son found guilty of two counts of rape
items.standfirst: Marius Borg Høiby, the 29-year-old son of Norway's Crown Princess Mette Marit, has been sentenced to four years in jail.
items.content: Marius Borg Høiby, the 29-year-old son of Norway's Crown Princess Mette Marit, has been sentenced to four years in jail.

items.title: Son of Norway’s crown princess convicted of rape and sentenced to four years in prison
items.standfirst: Marius Borg Høiby found guilty of ⁠two ​counts of rape, one count of domestic violence and other ⁠crimesMarius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, has been sentenced to four years in prison after being found guilty of several offences including two counts of rape.The verdict was handed down by the Oslo district court on Monday morning in a courthouse packed with journalists, nearly three months after Høiby’s closely watched six-week trial. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Son of Norway's crown princess sentenced to four years in prison for rape
items.standfirst: Marius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, was sentenced to four years in prison on Monday after an Oslo court convicted him of two counts of rape and 32 other offences, including violence against a former partner, threats and drug-related crimes.
items.content: Marius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, was sentenced to four years in prison on Monday after an Oslo court convicted him of two counts of rape and 32 other offences, including violence against a former partner, threats and drug-related crimes.

items.title: Norway braces for verdict in rape trial of crown princess's son Høiby
items.standfirst: Marius Borg Høiby will appear via video link, almost three months after his trial came to an end on 40 charges, including four counts of rape.
items.content: Marius Borg Høiby will appear via video link, almost three months after his trial came to an end on 40 charges, including four counts of rape.

clusters.title: Arrested protesters devastated after appeal court rules ban on Palestine Action is lawful
clusters.neutral_headline: Ban On Palestine Action Is Lawful

items.title: Arrested protesters devastated after appeal court rules ban on Palestine Action is lawful
items.standfirst: Proscription of direct action group has led to more than 700 people being charged under Terrorism ActProtesters arrested for allegedly supporting Palestine Action have expressed anger at the court of appeal’s decision that the ban on the direct action group was lawful.On Monday, five judges overturned the high court’s February ruling that proscription was unlawful, meaning that more than 3,000 people who have been arrested under the Terrorism Act since proscription, more than 700 of whom have been charged, could now face prosecution. Continue reading...
items.content: Proscription of direct action group has led to more than 700 people being charged under Terrorism ActProtesters arrested for allegedly supporting Palestine Action have expressed anger at the court of appeal’s decision that the ban on the direct action group was lawful.On Monday, five judges overturned the high court’s February ruling that proscription was unlawful, meaning that more than 3,000 people who have been arrested under the Terrorism Act since proscription, more than 700 of whom have been charged, could now face prosecution. Continue reading...

items.title: Ban on Palestine Action was lawful, court of appeal rules
items.standfirst: Judges overturn decision of high court that government proscription of group under Terrorism Act was wrongThe home secretary’s decision to ban Palestine Action was lawful, the court of appeal has ruled.A five-strong panel, including the two most senior judges in England and Wales, overturned February’s decision of the high court that the proscription of the direct action group, the first to be banned under the Terrorism Act, was wrong. Continue reading...
items.content: Judges overturn decision of high court that government proscription of group under Terrorism Act was wrongThe home secretary’s decision to ban Palestine Action was lawful, the court of appeal has ruled.A five-strong panel, including the two most senior judges in England and Wales, overturned February’s decision of the high court that the proscription of the direct action group, the first to be banned under the Terrorism Act, was wrong. Continue reading...

items.content: Britain's Court of Appeal ruled that the government was right to proscribe Palestine Action - what happens next?

items.title: Palestine Action ban is lawful, Court of Appeal rules
items.standfirst: The Home Office had challenged a High Court ruling that the group's proscription should be quashed.
items.content: The Home Office had challenged a High Court ruling that the group's proscription should be quashed.

items.title: UK Court of Appeal upholds ban on Palestine Action as ‘terrorist’ group
items.standfirst: Judge says the group's behaviour was not that of a non-violent, direct action organisation.
items.content: Judge says the group's behaviour was not that of a non-violent, direct action organisation.

clusters.neutral_headline: Oliver Tree Dies In Helicopter Crash

items.title: Brazil : Oliver Tree, “Missed You” singer, killed in helicopter crash
items.standfirst: American alt-pop singer and internet personality Oliver Tree was aboard one of the two helicopters that collided mid-air in Brazil, leaving six dead. The musician was known for hits such as "Miss You" and "Life Goes On." He had over 11 million monthly listeners on Spotify, with his top songs listened to over 700 million times.

items.title: US musician Oliver Tree, 32, killed in helicopter crash in Brazil
items.standfirst: Alternative singer and internet personality among six who died when two helicopters collided over Rio de JaneiroThe American musician Oliver Tree has died in a helicopter crash in Brazil at the age of 32, according to reports.Two helicopters collided over Rio de Janeiro on Sunday morning and crashed in the city’s western zone, killing all six people onboard, including Tree, several Brazilian media outlets reported. clusters.title: Swiss reject proposal to cap population at 10 million

items.title: Swiss reject proposal to cap population at 10 million
items.standfirst: Voters in Switzerland on Sunday narrowly rejected a divisive anti-immigration proposal to cap the country's population, which had sparked warnings of "chaos". Nearly 55 percent of Swiss voters rejected the initiative put forward by the hard-right Swiss People's Party (SVP), which sought to stop the Alpine nation's population -- currently 9.1 million -- going above 10 million before 2050.
items.content: Voters in Switzerland on Sunday narrowly rejected a divisive anti-immigration proposal to cap the country's population, which had sparked warnings of "chaos". Nearly 55 percent of Swiss voters rejected the initiative put forward by the hard-right Swiss People's Party (SVP), which sought to stop the Alpine nation's population -- currently 9.1 million -- going above 10 million before 2050.

items.title: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
items.standfirst: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.
items.content: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.

items.title: Swiss voters reject proposal to cap population at ten million
items.standfirst: Swiss voters have rejected a right-wing initiative that sought to limit the population to 10 million, with final results showing a 55% majority against it. The vote also came alongside approval of a civilian service reform, while debate over immigration and Switzerland’s ties with the EU remained central to the campaign. [swissinfo.ch]
items.content: Swiss voters have rejected a right-wing initiative that sought to limit the population to 10 million, with final results showing a 55% majority against it. The vote also came alongside approval of a civilian service reform, while debate over immigration and Switzerland’s ties with the EU remained central to the campaign. [swissinfo.ch]

ai_summary: Swiss voters reject proposal to cap population
Nearly 55 percent of voters reject initiative
Proposal sought to stop population going above 10 million
Initiative put forward by hard-right Swiss People's Party
Vote also included approval of civilian service reform

clusters.title: Missouri community mourns death of 11 skydivers in plane crash: ‘It shocked everybody’
clusters.neutral_headline: Eleven Skydivers Killed In Plane Crash

items.title: Missouri community mourns death of 11 skydivers in plane crash: ‘It shocked everybody’
items.standfirst: Eleven skydivers and a pilot were killed in Sunday crash as aircraft for Skydive Kansas City was taking off in ButlerThe Missouri skydiving community is mourning the loss of several of its members after a plane crash south of Kansas City killed 11 skydivers and a pilot.The crash occurred around noon on Sunday in Butler, Missouri, as an aircraft supporting operations for Skydive Kansas City was taking off, the company said in a statement on Monday, as reported by the local news outlet KCTV. Continue reading...
items.content: Eleven skydivers and a pilot were killed in Sunday crash as aircraft for Skydive Kansas City was taking off in ButlerThe Missouri skydiving community is mourning the loss of several of its members after a plane crash south of Kansas City killed 11 skydivers and a pilot.The crash occurred around noon on Sunday in Butler, Missouri, as an aircraft supporting operations for Skydive Kansas City was taking off, the company said in a statement on Monday, as reported by the local news outlet KCTV. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trump hosts "surreal" UFC fights at White House and makes history
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump Hosts UFC Fights At White House

items.title: Trump hosts "surreal" UFC fights at White House and makes history
items.standfirst: President Trump hosted a series of "UFC Freedom 250" mixed martial arts fights at the White House to celebrate America's 250th anniversary on his 80th birthday. CBS News' Weijia Jiang reports on the event and turnout.
items.content: President Trump hosted a series of "UFC Freedom 250" mixed martial arts fights at the White House to celebrate America's 250th anniversary on his 80th birthday. CBS News' Weijia Jiang reports on the event and turnout.

items.title: #Trump celebrates 80th birthday with #UFC fight at #WhiteHouse
items.standfirst: To kick-off celebrations for the United States' 250th anniversary, the #Trump administration hosted a #UFC fight in the White House garden. The $60 million event, which coincided with the US president's 80th birthday, came a few hours after Trump and Iranian officials announced they had reached a preliminary agreement to end the #war.
items.content: To kick-off celebrations for the United States' 250th anniversary, the #Trump administration hosted a #UFC fight in the White House garden.
